board__commission__committee_details.length_of_residency_in_the_county: Resident of Mariposa County since 2011 (approximately 13 years)

qualifications_and_experience.please_explain_why_you_wish_to_serve_on_:
I am applying to serve on the Mariposa County Planning Commission because, after more than a decade of living and working in the county, I feel a strong responsibility to help guide how our community grows while preserving the rural character and natural beauty that drew my family here in the first place. My professional background is in civil engineering and site planning, and for the past nine years I have worked locally with Sierra Foothills Design & Survey, Inc., where I focus on small-scale subdivision layouts, grading and drainage plans, and feasibility studies for both residential and light commercial projects in Mariposa and neighboring counties. 

In my day-to-day work, I regularly interact with the Mariposa County Planning Department, Public Works, and Building Department. I review and interpret the General Plan, zoning ordinances, subdivision regulations, and environmental review requirements (including CEQA) as part of my job. I have seen firsthand both the strengths of our current planning framework and the areas where clearer policies and better communication with residents and applicants could improve outcomes. Serving on the Planning Commission would allow me to bring that practical, on-the-ground perspective into the public decision-making process.

On a personal level, I care deeply about ensuring that growth in Mariposa County is thoughtful, transparent, and fair. My wife and I bought our home off Pine Ridge Road in 2013, after renting in town for two years. We navigated the permitting process to add a small accessory dwelling unit above our detached garage to accommodate my aging mother when her health declined. That experience gave me a very human understanding of how planning and zoning decisions affect ordinary families—timelines, costs, and the stress that can come from unclear expectations. I want to help ensure that our policies protect the environment and public safety while still being navigable for regular residents who may only go through the process once in their lives.

I am also motivated by concerns about wildfire risk, water availability, and the need to diversify our local economy beyond tourism while supporting it in a sustainable way. Over the last decade we have all seen increasingly extreme fire seasons and drought conditions. Through my work, I’ve been involved in projects that required careful consideration of defensible space, access for emergency vehicles, and impacts on wells and septic systems. I believe the Planning Commission plays a critical role in ensuring that new development is sited and designed with these realities in mind. I would like to contribute to policies and decisions that encourage fire-safe building patterns, protect our watersheds, and avoid creating long-term infrastructure burdens that the County and residents cannot realistically support.

Another reason I wish to serve is that I value open, respectful public dialogue. I have attended numerous Planning Commission and Board of Supervisors meetings over the years, both as a project representative and as a resident. I have seen contentious hearings about short-term rentals, cannabis regulations, and rural subdivision proposals. I believe I can help create a constructive environment where all sides feel heard, and where decisions are clearly tied back to adopted plans, ordinances, and factual evidence rather than emotion alone. In my professional role, I often find myself explaining technical issues to non-technical audiences—neighbors concerned about runoff, property owners worried about traffic, or clients trying to understand mitigation measures. I am patient and straightforward, and I do not mind taking extra time to walk someone through a staff report or a site plan so they understand what is being proposed and why staff is recommending certain conditions.

I also want to give back to a community that has been very good to my family. When we first moved here from Merced in 2011, we were looking for a quieter, safer place to raise our two children, who were then in elementary school. Mariposa County welcomed us—from the elementary school PTO, to youth soccer, to the library and local churches. Now that our kids are older (one at Fresno State and one at Mariposa County High School), I have more time to devote to public service. I see serving on the Planning Commission as a way to use my specific skills (technical reading of plans, understanding of infrastructure constraints, familiarity with state and county regulations) for the broader benefit of the county.

Finally, I am interested in helping bridge the gap between long-time residents and newer arrivals. I have friends and neighbors who have been here for generations, and others who relocated in the last five years from the Bay Area or Southern California. There can sometimes be tension around change, housing needs, and expectations regarding services and development. I appreciate the deep roots and history of Mariposa’s ranching, mining, and tourism traditions, and I also understand the pressures of housing affordability and the desire for modern amenities. I believe thoughtful, well-communicated planning decisions can help reduce conflict by setting clear expectations and ensuring that changes are incremental, consistent with the General Plan, and sensitive to the character of existing neighborhoods.

In summary, I want to serve on the Mariposa County Planning Commission because I:
- Have relevant professional experience in civil engineering, site design, and local permitting.
- Understand the County’s planning documents and regulatory framework from both the applicant and resident perspectives.
- Care deeply about balancing growth, environmental protection, wildfire safety, and water resource stewardship.
- Value transparent, respectful public processes and am comfortable listening to and weighing differing viewpoints.
- Am ready and able to commit the necessary time to thoroughly review staff reports, visit sites as needed, and attend all required meetings.

I see this as a serious responsibility, not just a title. If appointed, I would approach each agenda item with an open mind, a clear understanding of applicable laws and plans, and a commitment to fairness and the long-term well-being of Mariposa County.
qualifications_and_experience.please_list_prior_current_appointments_t:
Mariposa County Transportation Advisory Committee (2018–2022)
- Role: Member representing District 3
- Responsibilities: Reviewed proposed road improvement projects, advised on priority-setting for limited transportation funds, and provided feedback on pedestrian safety improvements in and around the town of Mariposa. I regularly read staff reports, attended quarterly meetings, and participated in two public workshops regarding Highway 140 corridor safety.
- Notable contributions: Helped advocate for improved signage and striping near school zones, and supported the inclusion of basic bike and pedestrian considerations in rural road improvement projects where feasible.

Mariposa County General Plan Update Citizen Workshop Series (Participant, not an appointed position) (2019–2020)
- Although not a formal appointment, I attended multiple evening workshops and breakout sessions related to the General Plan implementation and zoning code clean-up. I provided written comments related to rural residential lot sizes, wildfire evacuation routes, and minimum access standards for new subdivisions.

Sierra Foothills Habitat & Trails Working Group (Local volunteer advisory group, 2016–2019)
- Role: Volunteer member
- Responsibilities: Participated in informal coordination meetings between local trail advocates, property owners, and County staff to discuss potential trail connections and access improvements. While not an official County committee, we were occasionally asked to provide feedback on how proposed development might affect existing informal trail use or potential future trail corridors.

Mariposa Elementary School Site Council (2014–2017)
- Role: Parent representative
- Responsibilities: Reviewed and helped prioritize use of limited site funds, provided input on campus safety and traffic flow during drop-off and pick-up, and worked with the principal and teachers on school improvement plans. Although school-related, this role gave me experience reading formal reports, listening to community concerns, and making decisions in a public setting.

While I have not yet served on a formal land use decision-making body like the Planning Commission, these experiences have prepared me to work collaboratively with staff, other appointed members, and the public. They have also given me a realistic sense of the time commitment and the importance of being prepared, punctual, and respectful in all public meetings.
